description_long: This banner features a long, rectangular slab with a slightly raised perpendicular section near one end, giving it a T-shaped profile when viewed from above. The main body appears to be constructed from a solid, stone-like material with a visible horizontal band of reddish-brown running through its center, possibly indicating a layered or composite structure. The front face is detailed with various small metallic fixtures and embedded elements, suggesting it may serve a functional or decorative purpose in an urban environment. The surface is predominantly gray with a matte, slightly rough finish, and the overall construction looks robust and heavy. There are no visible graphics, text, or branding on the banner, making it appear blank and utilitarian. The support structure is integrated into the form, with no separate poles or frames visible.
description: A robust, T-shaped stone banner with a reddish-brown central band and metallic fixtures on the front, featuring a blank, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main face with metallic fixtures and embedded elements along the surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the banner and the perpendicular raised section.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length and the layered band running horizontally through the structure.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid, blocky profile and the raised section's depth.
